name: Barranquenho

ancestors.notes: European Portuguese: the dominant language surrounding Barrancos and the primary structural base of Barranquenho. The phonological system, orthographic conventions, and most of the lexicon derive from Portuguese.

Castilian Spanish: Barrancos is surrounded on three sides by the Spanish province of Huelva (Andalusia). Spanish influence is pervasive: betacism (v→b, as in Spanish), aspirated h (borrowed from Andalusian Spanish pronunciation), tch/ch affricate, alveolar trill r (not uvular ʁ as in Portuguese). The 2025 Convenção Ortográfica do Barranquenho codifies these features.
Galaico-Portuguese: shared nasal vowels, lh/nh digraph conventions, and general Ibero-Romance vowel quality inherited through the Portuguese base.

notes: Barranquenho, the contact vernacular of Barrancos (Baixo Alentejo, Portugal), spoken by ~1,600–2,000 people in a town surrounded on three sides by the Spanish province of Huelva. Classified as Extremaduran Portuguese (ext-PT) with heavy Spanish adstrate influence; listed by UNESCO as 'definitely endangered'. The 2025 Convenção Ortográfica do Barranquenho (II Congresso Barranquenho, June 2025) codified the orthographic norms implemented in this spec. Source: g2p-barranquenho phonemizer (https://github.com/NLP-Workspace/g2p-barranquenho), Convenção Ortográfica 2025. KEY PHONOLOGICAL FEATURES distinguishing Barranquenho from standard pt-PT: (1) BETACISM: /v/ merged with /b/ — the phoneme /v/ does not exist; ⟨v⟩ always represents /b/ (same as Spanish; unlike Portuguese where /v/=/v/). Comment in source: 'NOT USED in barranquenho, it always written B never V'. (2) ASPIRATED ⟨h⟩: unlike Portuguese (where h is silent), Barranquenho ⟨h⟩ = /h/ (under Spanish/Andalusian influence). (3) ALVEOLAR TRILL: word-initial ⟨r⟩ and ⟨rr⟩ = /r/ (alveolar trill), NOT /ʁ/ (uvular) as in standard European Portuguese — matching the Spanish pattern. (4) ⟨tch⟩ = /tʃ/: affricate trigraph absent from standard Portuguese orthography. (5) SIBILANT VOICING: ⟨s⟩ word-initial = /s/, intervocalic = /z/ (like Portuguese). (6) ⟨g⟩ + front vowel = /ʒ/ (like Portuguese, NOT /x/ as in Spanish/Rionorese). (7) VOWELS: word-final ⟨a⟩ → /ɐ/, word-final ⟨e⟩ → /ɨ/ (Portuguese-style vowel reduction maintained). Nasal vowels: ⟨en/em⟩ → /ẽj/ (Portuguese nasal diphthong). PHONEMES NOT PRESENT: /v/ (merged into /b/), /ʁ/ (replaced by alveolar /r/).

sources.author: Penny, R.
sources.title: A History of the Spanish Language
